comment: Advance widths MUST be one of the values in allowed_widths. iA Writer Quattro uses four canonical width classes (300/450/600/900) plus a few outlier widths on precomposed accent glyphs. Run 'scripts/inspect_font.py' on the source fonts to verify.

weight_remap._comment: Optional avar remap on the wght axis to make Regular render slightly heavier without redrawing outlines. `nudge` is the amount added to the axis default (400): clients requesting wght=400 will render as if at wght=400+nudge. Range ~15 (subtle) to ~40 (clearly heavier). Set enabled=false to disable.

modifications._comment: Map glyph names to new advance widths. All values must be in allowed_widths above. Run scripts/inspect.py to see current widths and glyph names.

remove_contours._comment: Delete specific contours from simple glyphs in place. `zero`, `zero.numr`, and `zero.dnom` all have 3 contours (outer ring, inner counter, center dot); dropping contour index 2 removes the dot so the default 0 renders empty and the rebuilt `percent` composite shows two empty small zeros around the slash. No precomposed fraction uses a zero digit, so this has no side effects on fractions.

cmap_aliases._comment: Map extra Unicode codepoints to existing glyphs. (Currently unused — U+2009 is handled by create_empty_glyphs below.)

create_empty_glyphs._comment: Create brand-new empty (no-ink) glyphs with a given advance width and alias Unicode codepoints to them. Used for U+2009 THIN SPACE which is intentionally an exception to allowed_widths — set to 150 (half the minimum class width of 300) so it renders as a narrow, barely-there gap. Key = new glyph name, value = { cp: hex, width: int }.

fraction_rebuild._comment: Replace precomposed fraction glyphs with composites of <num>.numr + fraction + <den>.dnom at advance_width. reshape_fraction_slash rewrites the `fraction` glyph itself as a single continuous slash (preserving its 8-point/2-contour gvar structure so the weight axis still works). layout.auto=true computes per-target x-offsets from actual component ink bounds so each composite is centered; layout.gap adds horizontal space between numerator ink end and denominator ink start (larger = wider fractions). digit_scale applies a uniform 2x2 transform to the .numr/.dnom components only (slash stays at 1.0 so its gvar deltas remain valid) — used to enlarge the small digits for readability without redrawing any outlines.
